metadata.name: CIS Critical Security Controls v8
metadata.description: The CIS Critical Security Controls (CIS Controls) are a prioritized set of actions that collectively form a defense-in-depth set of best practices to mitigate the most common attacks against systems and networks. Developed by a consensus community of cybersecurity experts, CIS Controls v8 has been updated to keep up with modern systems and software.
metadata.classification_reasoning: CIS Controls is a best-practice compliance framework providing prioritized security recommendations. While not requiring formal certification, it is widely adopted as a benchmark and is referenced by many regulatory frameworks.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide a prioritized, prescriptive set of cybersecurity best practices and defensive actions that can help prevent the most pervasive and dangerous attacks and support compliance with multiple legal, regulatory, and policy frameworks.
metadata.framework_scope: All organizations regardless of size or industry, with Implementation Groups (IG1, IG2, IG3) providing scalable adoption paths based on organizational risk profile and resources.
framework_objectives: Provide a risk-based approach to cybersecurity
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, IT security teams, system administrators, compliance officers, and organizations of all sizes seeking actionable cybersecurity guidance
adoption_approach: Assess current security posture against CIS Controls
Implement controls using CIS Benchmarks and tools
Measure and report on control effectiveness

sections: Control 01: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
Control 02: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
Control 04: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
Control 09: Email and Web Browser Protections
Control 14: Security Awareness and Skills Training

controls.title: Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
controls.description: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices, network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers.
controls.full_text: Conduct active and passive asset discovery. Include hardware address, network address, asset name, enterprise asset owner, department, and approved status. Review and update at least every two weeks or more frequently.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

controls.title: Address Unauthorized Assets
controls.description: Ensure that a process exists to address unauthorized assets on a weekly basis. Remove unauthorized assets from the network, deny the asset from connecting remotely, or quarantine the asset.
controls.full_text: The enterprise should have processes to handle newly discovered unauthorized assets. Actions include removing the unauthorized asset from the network, denying the asset from connecting to the network remotely, or placing the asset in a quarantine network segment.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

controls.title: Utilize an Active Discovery Tool
controls.description: Utilize an active discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network. Configure the active discovery tool to execute daily, or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Use an active discovery tool to identify enterprise assets connected to the network. Configure for daily execution and integrate results into the enterprise asset inventory.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

controls.title: Use Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) Logging
controls.description: Use DHCP logging on all DHCP servers or Internet Protocol (IP) address management tools to update the enterprise's asset inventory.
controls.full_text: Enable DHCP logging on all DHCP servers or use IP address management tools. Use logs to update the enterprise asset inventory and identify unauthorized assets.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

controls.title: Use a Passive Asset Discovery Tool
controls.description: Use a passive discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network. Review and use scans to update the enterprise's asset inventory.
controls.full_text: Deploy passive discovery tools to identify assets that may not respond to active scans. Integrate passive discovery results into the enterprise asset inventory.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
controls.description: Establish and maintain a detailed inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets. Review and update at least every two weeks.
controls.full_text: The software inventory should document the title, publisher, initial install/use date, business purpose, URL, app store, version, deployment mechanism, and decommission date for each entry. Review and update bi-weekly or more frequently.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
controls.description: Ensure that only currently supported software is designated as authorized in the software inventory. Unsupported software should be designated as unauthorized and tagged for removal.
controls.full_text: Review the software inventory to identify unsupported software. If unsupported software cannot be removed due to business necessity, document the risk acceptance and implement compensating controls.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Address Unauthorized Software
controls.description: Ensure that unauthorized software is either removed from enterprise assets or receive a documented exception. Review monthly or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Establish processes to remove unauthorized software from enterprise assets or obtain documented exceptions. Track unauthorized software discoveries and remediation actions.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Utilize Automated Software Inventory Tools
controls.description: Utilize software inventory tools, when possible, throughout the enterprise to automate the discovery and documentation of installed software.
controls.full_text: Deploy automated software inventory tools to discover and document installed software across all enterprise assets. Integrate results with the enterprise software inventory.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Allowlist Authorized Software
controls.description: Use technical controls, such as application allowlisting, to ensure that only authorized software can execute or be installed on enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Implement application allowlisting on enterprise assets to prevent unauthorized software from executing. Maintain the allowlist and review it regularly. Block unauthorized software from executing.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Allowlist Authorized Libraries
controls.description: Use technical controls to ensure that only authorized software libraries can load into a system process.
controls.full_text: Implement controls to restrict which software libraries can be loaded into system processes. This helps prevent DLL hijacking and other library-based attacks.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Allowlist Authorized Scripts
controls.description: Use technical controls, such as digital signatures and version control, to ensure that only authorized scripts can execute.
controls.full_text: Implement controls to ensure only authorized scripts can execute on enterprise assets. Use digital signatures, version control, and script execution policies.
controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain a data management process that addresses data sensitivity, data owner, handling of data, data retention limits, and disposal requirements.
controls.full_text: Create a data management process based on data sensitivity levels. Include data classification criteria, data owner responsibilities, handling procedures, retention periods, and disposal methods. Review and update annually or when significant changes occur.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory
controls.description: Establish and maintain a data inventory, based on the enterprise's data management process. Inventory sensitive data at a minimum.
controls.full_text: Create and maintain an inventory of sensitive data stored, processed, or transmitted by enterprise assets. Include data location, classification level, data owner, and handling requirements.

controls.title: Configure Data Access Control Lists
controls.description: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.
controls.full_text: Implement data access control lists (ACLs) based on the principle of least privilege and need-to-know. Apply ACLs consistently across file systems, databases, and applications.

controls.title: Enforce Data Retention
controls.description: Retain data according to the enterprise's data management process. Data retention must include both minimum and maximum timelines.
controls.full_text: Implement technical controls to enforce data retention timelines. Ensure data is retained for minimum required periods and disposed of after maximum retention periods.

controls.title: Securely Dispose of Data
controls.description: Securely dispose of data as outlined in the enterprise's data management process. Ensure the disposal process and method are commensurate with the data sensitivity.
controls.full_text: Implement secure data disposal methods appropriate to the sensitivity of the data. Methods include secure overwrite, degaussing, physical destruction, or cryptographic erasure.

controls.title: Encrypt Data on End-User Devices
controls.description: Encrypt data on end-user devices containing sensitive data. Example implementations include Windows BitLocker, Apple FileVault, or Linux dm-crypt.
controls.full_text: Deploy full-disk or file-level encryption on all end-user devices that store or process sensitive data. Manage encryption keys centrally.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Classification Scheme
controls.description: Establish and maintain an overall data classification scheme for the enterprise. The classification scheme should be based on the enterprise's data management process.
controls.full_text: Define data classification levels (e.g., public, internal, confidential, restricted) with clear criteria and handling requirements for each level.

controls.title: Document Data Flows
controls.description: Document data flows. Data flow documentation includes service provider data flows and should be based on the enterprise's data management process.
controls.full_text: Map and document how sensitive data flows through the enterprise, including external service providers. Update data flow documentation when significant changes occur.

controls.title: Encrypt Data on Removable Media
controls.description: Encrypt data on removable media.
controls.full_text: Implement encryption requirements for all sensitive data stored on removable media such as USB drives, external hard drives, and optical media.

controls.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit
controls.description: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations include Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).
controls.full_text: Ensure all sensitive data transmitted across networks is encrypted using approved protocols such as TLS 1.2+, SSH, or IPSec VPN.

controls.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest
controls.description: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases containing sensitive data.
controls.full_text: Implement encryption for sensitive data stored on servers, in databases, and within applications. Use industry-standard encryption algorithms and manage keys securely.

controls.title: Segment Data Processing and Storage Based on Sensitivity
controls.description: Segment data processing and storage based on the sensitivity of the data. Do not process sensitive data on enterprise assets or network segments that also serve lower sensitivity data.
controls.full_text: Implement network and system segmentation to separate processing and storage environments based on data sensitivity levels. Prevent commingling of data with different classification levels.

controls.title: Deploy a Data Loss Prevention Solution
controls.description: Implement a data loss prevention (DLP) solution on enterprise assets to monitor and protect against unauthorized data transfers.
controls.full_text: Deploy DLP solutions to detect and prevent unauthorized transfer of sensitive data. Configure policies based on data classification and monitor for policy violations.

controls.title: Log Sensitive Data Access
controls.description: Log sensitive data access, including modification and disposal.
controls.full_text: Implement logging for all access to sensitive data repositories. Capture access, modification, and disposal events. Review logs for unauthorized access patterns.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for enterprise assets and software. Review and update documentation annually.
controls.full_text: Develop a formal secure configuration process covering the full lifecycle from initial build through decommission. Include configuration baselines, hardening guides, and exception processes.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process for Network Infrastructure
controls.description: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for network infrastructure. Review and update documentation annually.
controls.full_text: Develop secure configuration baselines for all network infrastructure devices including routers, switches, firewalls, and wireless access points.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Configure Automatic Session Locking on Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Configure automatic session locking on enterprise assets after a defined period of inactivity. For general purpose operating systems, the period must not exceed 15 minutes.
controls.full_text: Configure screen lock/session timeout on all enterprise assets. Maximum 15 minutes for general purpose OS. Ensure re-authentication is required to unlock.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Implement and Manage a Firewall on Servers
controls.description: Implement and manage a firewall on servers, where supported. Example implementations include a virtual firewall, operating system firewall, or a third-party firewall agent.
controls.full_text: Deploy and configure host-based firewalls on all servers. Define and enforce firewall rules based on the principle of least privilege. Monitor and maintain firewall configurations.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Implement and Manage a Firewall on End-User Devices
controls.description: Implement and manage a host-based firewall or port-filtering tool on end-user devices with a default-deny rule.
controls.full_text: Deploy host-based firewalls on all end-user devices. Configure with a default-deny rule that blocks all traffic except explicitly authorized connections.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Securely Manage Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Securely manage enterprise assets and software. Example implementations include managing configuration through version-controlled infrastructure-as-code.
controls.full_text: Use secure management practices for enterprise assets including change management, version control, and infrastructure-as-code where applicable.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software, such as root, administrator, and other pre-configured vendor accounts.
controls.full_text: Change, disable, or remove default accounts and passwords on all enterprise assets. Document any exceptions with compensating controls.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Uninstall or disable unnecessary services on enterprise assets and software, such as an unused file sharing service, web application module, or service function.
controls.full_text: Identify and remove or disable unnecessary services, ports, protocols, and features on all enterprise assets. Maintain a list of approved services.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Configure Trusted DNS Servers on Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Configure trusted DNS servers on enterprise assets. Example implementations include configuring assets to use enterprise-controlled DNS servers.
controls.full_text: Configure all enterprise assets to use organization-controlled or trusted DNS servers. Block direct DNS queries to external DNS servers.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Enforce Automatic Device Lockout on Portable End-User Devices
controls.description: Enforce automatic device lockout following a predetermined threshold of local failed authentication attempts on portable end-user devices.
controls.full_text: Configure automatic device lockout after a defined number of failed authentication attempts on mobile devices and laptops. Define lockout duration and reset procedures.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Enforce Remote Wipe Capability on Portable End-User Devices
controls.description: Remotely wipe enterprise data from enterprise-owned portable end-user devices when deemed appropriate such as lost or stolen devices.
controls.full_text: Implement remote wipe capability for all portable enterprise devices. Document the process for initiating remote wipes and maintain logs of wipe actions.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Separate Enterprise Workspaces on Mobile End-User Devices
controls.description: Ensure separate enterprise workspaces are used on mobile end-user devices, where supported.
controls.full_text: Implement containerization or workspace separation on mobile devices to separate enterprise data and applications from personal data. Enforce through MDM solutions.
controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
controls.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of all accounts managed in the enterprise. Include user, administrator, and service accounts.
controls.full_text: Create and maintain an inventory of all accounts including username, associated enterprise asset, account owner, account purpose, and account status. Review and validate at least quarterly.

controls.title: Use Unique Passwords
controls.description: Use unique passwords for all enterprise assets. Best practice implementation includes, at a minimum, an 8-character password for accounts using MFA and a 14-character password for accounts not using MFA.
controls.full_text: Enforce unique password requirements across all enterprise accounts. Minimum 8 characters with MFA or 14 characters without MFA. Prohibit password reuse across accounts.

controls.title: Disable Dormant Accounts
controls.description: Delete or disable any dormant accounts after a period of 45 days of inactivity, where supported.
controls.full_text: Implement automated processes to identify and disable dormant accounts after 45 days of inactivity. Review disabled accounts and remove if no longer needed.

controls.title: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
controls.description: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Ensure administrative tasks are performed only from dedicated administrator accounts, separate from standard user accounts. Prohibit daily use of administrator accounts for non-administrative tasks.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Accounts
controls.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of service accounts. Include a description of the service, associated enterprise asset, account owner, and data classification.
controls.full_text: Document all service accounts with their purpose, associated systems, owners, data classification, and authentication method. Review at least quarterly.

controls.title: Centralize Account Management
controls.description: Centralize account management through a directory or identity service.
controls.full_text: Implement centralized account management using directory services (e.g., Active Directory, LDAP) or identity-as-a-service providers. Integrate all enterprise assets with centralized identity management.

controls.title: Establish an Access Granting Process
controls.description: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for granting access to enterprise assets upon new hire, rights grant, or role change.
controls.full_text: Define and implement a formal process for granting access to enterprise assets. Include request, approval, provisioning, and documentation steps. Automate where possible.

controls.title: Establish an Access Revoking Process
controls.description: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for revoking access to enterprise assets, through disabling accounts, upon termination, rights revocation, or role change.
controls.full_text: Define and implement a formal process for revoking access upon termination, transfer, or role change. Include timely disablement/removal of access and return of physical credentials.

controls.title: Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
controls.description: Require all externally-exposed enterprise or third-party applications to enforce MFA, where supported.
controls.full_text: Implement multi-factor authentication for all externally-accessible applications and services. Acceptable factors include hardware tokens, software tokens, biometrics, or push notifications.

controls.title: Require MFA for Remote Network Access
controls.description: Require MFA for remote network access.
controls.full_text: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all remote access connections including VPN, remote desktop, and similar technologies.

controls.title: Require MFA for Administrative Access
controls.description: Require MFA for all administrative access accounts, where supported, on all enterprise assets, whether managed on-site or through a third-party provider.
controls.full_text: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all administrative/privileged accounts. Include both on-premises and cloud-based administrative interfaces.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Authentication and Authorization Systems
controls.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of the enterprise's authentication and authorization systems.
controls.full_text: Document all authentication and authorization systems used in the enterprise. Include identity providers, directory services, SSO solutions, PAM solutions, and MFA services.

controls.title: Centralize Access Control
controls.description: Centralize access control for all enterprise assets through a directory service or SSO provider.
controls.full_text: Implement centralized access control using directory services, SSO, or federated identity management. Integrate all enterprise applications with centralized access control where possible.

controls.title: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
controls.description: Define and maintain role-based access control, through determining and documenting the access rights necessary for each role within the enterprise.
controls.full_text: Define roles with associated access privileges based on job responsibilities. Document role definitions, associated permissions, and review/update regularly. Implement RBAC technically.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain a documented vulnerability management process for enterprise assets. Review and update documentation annually.
controls.full_text: Create a formal vulnerability management process covering identification, analysis, prioritization, remediation, and verification. Define SLAs for remediation based on severity.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain a risk-based remediation strategy documented in a remediation process.
controls.full_text: Define a risk-based approach to vulnerability remediation with priority levels, acceptable remediation timelines, and exception handling procedures.

controls.title: Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
controls.description: Perform operating system updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management on a monthly basis, or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Implement automated OS patch management for all enterprise assets. Deploy patches at minimum monthly, with critical patches deployed more rapidly based on risk assessment.

controls.title: Perform Automated Application Patch Management
controls.description: Perform application updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management on a monthly basis, or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Implement automated application patch management for all enterprise software. Deploy patches at minimum monthly, with critical patches deployed more rapidly.

controls.title: Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Perform automated vulnerability scans of internal enterprise assets on a quarterly basis, or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Deploy and configure vulnerability scanning tools to automatically scan all internal enterprise assets. Run scans at least quarterly and after significant changes.

controls.title: Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Externally-Exposed Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Perform automated vulnerability scans of externally-exposed enterprise assets on a quarterly basis or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Configure vulnerability scanning for all externally-exposed assets including web applications, public-facing servers, and network devices. Scan at least quarterly.

controls.title: Remediate Detected Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Remediate detected vulnerabilities in software through processes and tooling on a monthly, or more frequent, basis.
controls.full_text: Track and remediate all detected vulnerabilities within defined SLA timelines. Document remediation actions, compensating controls, or risk acceptances.
evidence_requirements: Risk acceptance documentation for unresolved vulnerabilities

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain an audit log management process that defines the enterprise's logging requirements.
controls.full_text: Define logging requirements including what events to log, log format, retention periods, review frequency, and storage requirements. Document and maintain the process.

controls.title: Collect Audit Logs
controls.description: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Enable and collect audit logs from all enterprise assets including servers, workstations, network devices, and applications. Ensure consistent log format and centralized collection.

controls.title: Ensure Adequate Audit Log Storage
controls.description: Ensure that logging destinations maintain adequate storage to comply with the enterprise's audit log management process.
controls.full_text: Provision adequate storage for audit logs based on log volume, retention requirements, and growth projections. Monitor storage utilization and alert when thresholds are reached.

controls.title: Standardize Time Synchronization
controls.description: Standardize time synchronization. Configure at least two synchronized time sources across enterprise assets, where supported.
controls.full_text: Configure NTP or similar time synchronization across all enterprise assets using at least two reliable time sources. Ensure accurate timestamps for forensic analysis.

controls.title: Collect Detailed Audit Logs
controls.description: Configure detailed audit logging for enterprise assets containing sensitive data. Include event source, date, username, timestamp, source/destination addresses, and other useful elements.
controls.full_text: Enable detailed logging on assets containing sensitive data. Capture comprehensive event data including source, date, username, timestamps, source/destination addresses, and relevant metadata.

controls.title: Collect DNS Query Audit Logs
controls.description: Collect DNS query audit logs on enterprise assets, where appropriate and supported.
controls.full_text: Enable DNS query logging on DNS servers and endpoints where supported. Use DNS logs for security monitoring and incident investigation.

controls.title: Collect URL Request Audit Logs
controls.description: Collect URL request audit logs on enterprise assets, where appropriate and supported.
controls.full_text: Enable URL request logging through web proxies, firewalls, or endpoint solutions. Use logs for security monitoring, policy enforcement, and incident investigation.

controls.title: Collect Command-Line Audit Logs
controls.description: Collect command-line audit logs. Example implementations include collecting audit logs from PowerShell, BASH, and remote administrative terminals.
controls.full_text: Enable command-line logging on servers and critical workstations. Capture PowerShell, Bash, and other command-line activities for security monitoring.

controls.title: Centralize Audit Logs
controls.description: Centralize, to the extent possible, audit log collection and retention across enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Implement centralized log management using a SIEM or log aggregation platform. Forward logs from all enterprise assets to the central platform.

controls.title: Retain Audit Logs
controls.description: Retain audit logs across enterprise assets for a minimum of 90 days.
controls.full_text: Retain audit logs for a minimum of 90 days with online storage and longer periods in archive storage as required by regulatory or business requirements.

controls.title: Conduct Audit Log Reviews
controls.description: Conduct reviews of audit logs to detect anomalies or abnormal events that could indicate a potential threat.
controls.full_text: Perform regular reviews of audit logs to identify security events, anomalies, and potential threats. Use automated tools and manual review processes.

controls.title: Collect Service Provider Logs
controls.description: Collect service provider logs, where supported. Example implementations include collecting authentication and authorization events, data creation and disposal events, and user management events.
controls.full_text: Collect and integrate logs from cloud service providers and other third-party services. Include authentication events, administrative actions, and data access events.

controls.title: Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browsers and Email Clients
controls.description: Ensure only fully supported browsers and email clients are allowed to execute in the enterprise.
controls.full_text: Standardize on fully supported browser and email client versions. Remove or disable unsupported versions and configure automatic updates.

controls.title: Use DNS Filtering Services
controls.description: Use DNS filtering services on all enterprise assets to block access to known malicious domains.
controls.full_text: Implement DNS-based filtering to block access to known malicious, phishing, and inappropriate domains. Configure for all enterprise assets.

controls.title: Maintain and Enforce Network-Based URL Filters
controls.description: Enforce and update network-based URL filters to limit an enterprise asset from connecting to potentially malicious or unapproved websites.
controls.full_text: Deploy web proxy or URL filtering solutions to control web access. Maintain category-based filtering policies and block known malicious URLs.

controls.title: Restrict Unnecessary or Unauthorized Browser and Email Client Extensions
controls.description: Restrict, either through uninstalling or disabling, any unauthorized or unnecessary browser or email client plugins, extensions, and add-on applications.
controls.full_text: Implement controls to restrict browser extensions and email plugins to only approved ones. Regularly audit installed extensions.

controls.title: Implement DMARC
controls.description: To lower the chance of spoofed or modified emails from valid domains, implement DMARC policy and verification.
controls.full_text: Configure DMARC, SPF, and DKIM records for all enterprise email domains. Set DMARC policy to quarantine or reject.

controls.title: Block Unnecessary File Types
controls.description: Block unnecessary file types attempting to enter the enterprise's email gateway.
controls.full_text: Configure email gateways to block dangerous file types and extensions. Maintain an updated list of blocked file types based on threat intelligence.
evidence_requirements: Email gateway file type blocking configuration

controls.title: Deploy and Maintain Email Server Anti-Malware Protections
controls.description: Deploy and maintain email server anti-malware protections, such as attachment scanning and/or sandboxing.
controls.full_text: Implement anti-malware scanning on email servers including attachment scanning, URL scanning, and sandboxing for suspicious attachments.

controls.title: Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
controls.description: Deploy and maintain anti-malware software on all enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Install and maintain anti-malware software on all enterprise assets including servers and workstations. Ensure real-time protection is enabled and definitions are current.

controls.title: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Signature Updates
controls.description: Configure automatic updates for anti-malware signature files on all enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Configure anti-malware solutions to automatically download and apply signature updates. Verify updates are being applied across all managed assets.

controls.title: Disable Autorun and Autoplay for Removable Media
controls.description: Disable autorun and autoplay auto-execute functionality for removable media.
controls.full_text: Disable autorun and autoplay functionality on all enterprise assets to prevent automatic execution of potentially malicious code from removable media.

controls.title: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Scanning of Removable Media
controls.description: Configure anti-malware software to automatically scan removable media.
controls.full_text: Configure anti-malware solutions to automatically scan removable media when connected to enterprise assets.

controls.title: Enable Anti-Exploitation Features
controls.description: Enable anti-exploitation features on enterprise assets and software, where possible.
controls.full_text: Enable anti-exploitation features such as DEP, ASLR, EMET/Exploit Guard, and application-level protections on all enterprise assets.

controls.title: Centrally Manage Anti-Malware Software
controls.description: Centrally manage anti-malware software.
controls.full_text: Use centralized management for anti-malware solutions to ensure consistent policy application, signature updates, and visibility across all managed assets.

controls.title: Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software
controls.description: Use behavior-based anti-malware software.
controls.full_text: Deploy behavior-based or next-generation anti-malware solutions that can detect unknown threats through behavioral analysis, machine learning, and heuristic detection.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain a data recovery process.
controls.full_text: Develop a formal data recovery process including backup scope, frequency, retention periods, recovery procedures, and testing requirements.

controls.title: Perform Automated Backups
controls.description: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups on a weekly, or more frequent, basis.
controls.full_text: Implement automated backup solutions for all in-scope enterprise assets. Ensure backups run at least weekly and more frequently for critical data.

controls.title: Protect Recovery Data
controls.description: Protect recovery data with equivalent controls as the original data. Reference encryption or data separation, based on requirements.
controls.full_text: Apply appropriate protection to backup and recovery data including encryption, access controls, and physical security equivalent to the original data.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
controls.description: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data using offline or immutable backups.
controls.full_text: Maintain at least one instance of recovery data that is isolated from the production environment using air-gapped storage, immutable backups, or cloud-based isolated vaults.

controls.title: Test Data Recovery
controls.description: Test backup recovery quarterly, or more frequently, for a sampling of in-scope enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Perform regular backup recovery testing to verify that backups are viable and that the recovery process works correctly. Test at least quarterly.

controls.title: Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
controls.description: Ensure network infrastructure is kept up-to-date. Example implementations include running the latest stable release of software.
controls.full_text: Maintain current firmware and software versions on all network infrastructure devices. Apply security patches and updates in accordance with the vulnerability management process.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
controls.description: Establish and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture must address segmentation, least privilege, and availability.
controls.full_text: Design and maintain a network architecture incorporating security principles including segmentation, DMZ, least privilege access, and redundancy for availability.

controls.title: Securely Manage Network Infrastructure
controls.description: Securely manage network infrastructure. Example implementations include version-controlled infrastructure-as-code.
controls.full_text: Manage network infrastructure using secure practices including change management, out-of-band management, version control, and access restrictions.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain Architecture Diagram(s)
controls.description: Establish and maintain architecture diagram(s) and/or other network documentation.
controls.full_text: Create and maintain detailed network architecture diagrams including network topology, data flows, DMZ boundaries, and security control placement. Update diagrams when changes occur.

controls.title: Centralize Network Authentication, Authorization, and Auditing (AAA)
controls.description: Centralize network AAA.
controls.full_text: Implement centralized AAA services (e.g., RADIUS, TACACS+) for network infrastructure device management. Ensure all network device authentication goes through centralized services.

controls.title: Use of Secure Network Management and Communication Protocols
controls.description: Use secure network management and communication protocols. Example implementations include 802.1X, Wi-Fi Protected Access 3 (WPA3), and Transport Layer Security (TLS).
controls.full_text: Use encrypted and authenticated protocols for network management and communications. Replace insecure protocols (Telnet, HTTP, SNMPv1/v2c) with secure alternatives.

controls.title: Ensure Remote Devices Utilize a VPN and are Connecting to an Enterprise's AAA Infrastructure
controls.description: Require users to authenticate to enterprise-managed VPN and AAA infrastructure prior to accessing enterprise resources.
controls.full_text: Require remote devices to connect through enterprise-managed VPN before accessing resources. Integrate VPN authentication with centralized AAA infrastructure.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain Dedicated Computing Resources for All Administrative Work
controls.description: Establish and maintain dedicated computing resources, either physically or logically separated, for all administrative tasks.
controls.full_text: Provision dedicated workstations or jump servers for administrative tasks, separated from general-purpose computing. Implement privileged access workstations (PAWs).

controls.title: Centralize Security Event Alerting
controls.description: Centralize security event alerting across enterprise assets for log correlation and analysis.
controls.full_text: Deploy a SIEM or equivalent solution to centralize security event alerting. Correlate events across multiple sources for comprehensive threat detection.

controls.title: Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Detection Solution
controls.description: Deploy a host-based intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Install host-based IDS/IPS on enterprise assets to detect and alert on suspicious activity at the host level.

controls.title: Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection Solution
controls.description: Deploy a network intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Deploy network IDS at strategic network points to monitor traffic for malicious activity and generate alerts.

controls.title: Perform Traffic Filtering Between Network Segments
controls.description: Perform traffic filtering between network segments, where appropriate.
controls.full_text: Implement traffic filtering between network segments using firewalls, ACLs, or similar controls. Follow the principle of least privilege for inter-segment communication.

controls.title: Manage Access Control for Remote Assets
controls.description: Manage access control for assets remotely connecting to enterprise resources.
controls.full_text: Implement network access control for remote devices connecting to enterprise resources. Verify device compliance before granting network access.

controls.title: Collect Network Traffic Flow Logs
controls.description: Collect network traffic flow logs and/or network traffic to review and alert upon from network devices.
controls.full_text: Enable NetFlow, sFlow, or similar traffic flow logging on network devices. Forward flow data to centralized analysis tools.

controls.title: Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Prevention Solution
controls.description: Deploy a host-based intrusion prevention solution on enterprise assets.
controls.full_text: Deploy HIPS on enterprise assets to automatically block and prevent detected malicious activity at the host level.

controls.title: Deploy a Network Intrusion Prevention Solution
controls.description: Deploy a network intrusion prevention solution.
controls.full_text: Deploy inline network IPS to automatically block malicious network traffic. Tune IPS rules to minimize false positives while maintaining detection capability.

controls.title: Deploy Port-Level Access Control
controls.description: Deploy port-level access control. Port-level access control utilizes 802.1X, or similar network access control (NAC) protocols.
controls.full_text: Implement 802.1X or equivalent port-level access control to authenticate devices before granting network port access.

controls.title: Perform Application Layer Filtering
controls.description: Perform application layer filtering. Example implementations include a filtering proxy, application layer firewall, or gateway.
controls.full_text: Deploy application-layer filtering to inspect and control traffic based on application protocols. Use web application firewalls and application proxies.

controls.title: Tune Security Event Alerting Thresholds
controls.description: Tune security event alerting thresholds monthly, or more frequently.
controls.full_text: Regularly review and adjust security alert thresholds to reduce false positives while maintaining detection of genuine threats. Document tuning decisions.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
controls.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program. Update training content annually or when significant enterprise changes occur.
controls.full_text: Develop a comprehensive security awareness program covering key topics. Deliver training to all users and update content at least annually.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
controls.description: Train workforce members to recognize social engineering attacks, such as phishing, pre-texting, and tailgating.
controls.full_text: Include social engineering awareness in training programs. Cover phishing, vishing, smishing, pretexting, baiting, and physical social engineering tactics.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
controls.description: Train workforce members on authentication best practices.
controls.full_text: Train all users on password best practices, MFA usage, and how to protect authentication credentials.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members on Data Handling Best Practices
controls.description: Train workforce members on how to identify and properly store, transfer, archive, and destroy sensitive data.
controls.full_text: Train users on data classification, handling procedures, secure data transfer, archiving, and secure disposal based on data sensitivity levels.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members on Causes of Unintentional Data Exposure
controls.description: Train workforce members to be aware of causes of unintentional data exposure.
controls.full_text: Train users to recognize and avoid common causes of data exposure including misconfigured sharing, accidental email to wrong recipients, and improper use of cloud services.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents
controls.description: Train workforce members to be able to recognize a potential incident and be able to report such an incident.
controls.full_text: Train all users to recognize security incidents and provide clear reporting channels and procedures.

controls.title: Train Workforce on How to Identify and Report if Their Enterprise Assets are Missing Security Updates
controls.description: Train workforce to understand how to verify and report out-of-date software patches.
controls.full_text: Train users to identify when their systems are missing security updates and how to report this to IT or security teams.

controls.title: Train Workforce on the Dangers of Connecting to and Transmitting Data over Insecure Networks
controls.description: Train workforce members on the dangers of connecting and transmitting data over insecure networks.
controls.full_text: Train users about risks of public Wi-Fi, unsecured networks, and the importance of using VPN and encrypted communications.

controls.title: Conduct Role-Specific Security Awareness and Skills Training
controls.description: Conduct role-specific security awareness and skills training.
controls.full_text: Develop and deliver specialized security training for specific roles including developers, system administrators, and security personnel.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
controls.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of service providers. Include classification, contact information, and service scope.
controls.full_text: Create and maintain an inventory of all service providers including cloud services, MSPs, SaaS, and outsourced functions. Include data classification and service scope.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Service Provider Management Policy
controls.description: Establish and maintain a service provider management policy. Ensure the policy addresses the classification, inventory, assessment, monitoring, and decommissioning of service providers.
controls.full_text: Develop a comprehensive service provider management policy covering the full provider lifecycle from onboarding through decommissioning.

controls.title: Classify Service Providers
controls.description: Classify service providers. Classification consideration may include one or more characteristics, such as data sensitivity, data volume, availability requirements, applicable regulations, inherent risk, and mitigated risk.
controls.full_text: Classify service providers based on risk factors including data sensitivity, criticality, regulatory requirements, and access to enterprise systems.

controls.title: Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements
controls.description: Ensure service provider contracts include security requirements.
controls.full_text: Include specific security, privacy, and compliance requirements in all service provider contracts. Address incident notification, data protection, and audit rights.

controls.title: Assess Service Providers
controls.description: Assess service providers consistent with the enterprise's service provider management policy.
controls.full_text: Conduct security assessments of service providers based on their risk classification. Review SOC reports, certifications, and conduct questionnaire-based assessments.

controls.title: Monitor Service Providers
controls.description: Monitor service providers consistent with the enterprise's service provider management policy.
controls.full_text: Implement ongoing monitoring of service providers including security posture, compliance status, and performance against security requirements.

controls.title: Securely Decommission Service Providers
controls.description: Securely decommission service providers. Example considerations include user and service account deactivation, termination of data flows, and secure disposal of enterprise data.
controls.full_text: Develop and follow a process for securely decommissioning service providers including revoking access, retrieving data, confirming data destruction, and closing connections.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain a secure application development process.
controls.full_text: Develop a secure SDLC process that integrates security activities throughout the development lifecycle including requirements, design, coding, testing, and deployment.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Process to Accept and Address Software Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Establish and maintain a process to accept and address reports of software vulnerabilities, including providing a means for external entities to report.
controls.full_text: Implement a vulnerability disclosure process including responsible disclosure policy, vulnerability intake mechanisms, and response procedures.

controls.title: Perform Root Cause Analysis on Security Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Perform root cause analysis on security vulnerabilities. Document lessons learned from the analysis.
controls.full_text: Conduct root cause analysis for security vulnerabilities found in applications. Document findings and implement systemic improvements.

controls.title: Establish and Manage an Inventory of Third-Party Software Components
controls.description: Establish and manage an updated inventory of third-party components used in development (SBOM).
controls.full_text: Create and maintain a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for all applications. Track third-party libraries, their versions, and known vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Use Up-to-Date and Trusted Third-Party Software Components
controls.description: Use up-to-date and trusted third-party software components.
controls.full_text: Ensure all third-party software components are from trusted sources, are current versions, and do not contain known vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Severity Rating System and Process for Application Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Establish and maintain a severity rating system for application vulnerabilities that facilitates prioritizing the order in which discovered vulnerabilities are fixed.
controls.full_text: Define a severity rating system for application vulnerabilities (e.g., CVSS-based) to prioritize remediation efforts.

controls.title: Use Standard Hardening Configuration Templates for Application Infrastructure
controls.description: Use standard, industry-recommended hardening configuration templates for application infrastructure components.
controls.full_text: Apply hardening templates and security benchmarks (e.g., CIS Benchmarks) to application infrastructure including web servers, application servers, and databases.

controls.title: Separate Production and Non-Production Systems
controls.description: Maintain separate environments for production, development, and testing.
controls.full_text: Maintain separate production, development, testing, and staging environments. Prevent production data from being used in non-production environments without sanitization.

controls.title: Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding
controls.description: Ensure all software development personnel receive training in writing secure code for their specific development environment and responsibilities.
controls.full_text: Provide secure coding training to all developers. Cover OWASP Top 10, language-specific security patterns, and secure development practices.

controls.title: Apply Secure Design Principles in Application Architectures
controls.description: Apply secure design principles in application architectures. Include least privilege, defense in depth, fail secure, security defaults, zero trust, and privacy by design.
controls.full_text: Ensure application architectures incorporate security design principles from the design phase. Conduct security architecture reviews.

controls.title: Leverage Vetted Modules or Services for Application Security Components
controls.description: Leverage vetted modules or services for application security components such as identity management, encryption, and auditing.
controls.full_text: Use proven, vetted security components and libraries for authentication, encryption, input validation, and other security functions rather than custom implementations.

controls.title: Implement Code-Level Security Checks
controls.description: Apply static and dynamic analysis tools within the application lifecycle to verify that secure coding practices are being followed.
controls.full_text: Integrate SAST and DAST tools into the CI/CD pipeline. Scan code for vulnerabilities during development and before deployment.

controls.title: Conduct Application Penetration Testing
controls.description: Conduct application penetration testing.
controls.full_text: Perform penetration testing on applications to identify vulnerabilities not found through automated scanning. Include authentication testing, business logic testing, and API security testing.

controls.title: Conduct Threat Modeling
controls.description: Conduct threat modeling during the design and development of applications.
controls.full_text: Perform threat modeling during application design to identify potential threats, attack vectors, and required countermeasures. Use frameworks like STRIDE or PASTA.

controls.title: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
controls.description: Designate one key person, and at least one backup, who will manage the enterprise's incident handling process.
controls.full_text: Assign primary and backup incident handlers with clear roles and responsibilities. Ensure 24/7 coverage for incident response.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
controls.description: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents.
controls.full_text: Maintain current contact lists for internal stakeholders, law enforcement, regulatory bodies, and other parties to be notified during incidents.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
controls.description: Establish and maintain an enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents.
controls.full_text: Implement a clear and accessible process for employees to report suspected security incidents. Include multiple reporting channels.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
controls.description: Establish and maintain an incident response process that addresses roles and responsibilities, compliance requirements, and a communication plan.
controls.full_text: Develop a comprehensive incident response process covering detection, analysis, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident activities.

controls.title: Assign Key Roles and Responsibilities
controls.description: Assign key roles and responsibilities for incident response, including staff from legal, IT, information security, facilities, public relations, human resources, incident responders, and analysts.
controls.full_text: Define and assign incident response roles across all relevant functions including technical, legal, communications, and management teams.

controls.title: Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Response
controls.description: Determine which primary and secondary mechanisms will be used to communicate and report during a security incident.
controls.full_text: Establish secure communication channels for incident response that remain available even if primary systems are compromised.

controls.title: Conduct Routine Incident Response Exercises
controls.description: Plan and conduct routine incident response exercises and scenarios for key personnel involved in the incident response process.
controls.full_text: Conduct regular tabletop exercises, simulations, and drills to test incident response capabilities. Include various scenario types.

controls.title: Conduct Post-Incident Reviews
controls.description: Conduct post-incident reviews to determine the root cause of incidents, develop and implement remediations, and share lessons learned.
controls.full_text: Perform post-incident reviews for all significant incidents. Document root causes, timeline, impact, and improvement recommendations.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain Security Incident Thresholds
controls.description: Establish and maintain security incident thresholds, including, at a minimum, differentiating between an incident and an event.
controls.full_text: Define thresholds for differentiating security events from incidents and for escalation levels. Include criteria for declaring major incidents.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
controls.description: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, and maturity of the enterprise.
controls.full_text: Develop a penetration testing program defining scope, frequency, methodology, and reporting requirements. Align with industry standards.

controls.title: Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
controls.description: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually.
controls.full_text: Conduct external penetration tests at least annually targeting internet-facing assets. Use qualified testers and follow established methodology.

controls.title: Remediate Penetration Test Findings
controls.description: Remediate penetration test findings based on the enterprise's policy for remediation scope and prioritization.
controls.full_text: Track and remediate all findings from penetration tests within defined timelines based on severity. Verify remediation through retesting.

controls.title: Validate Security Measures
controls.description: Validate security measures after each penetration test. If deemed necessary, modify rulesets and capabilities to detect the techniques used during testing.
controls.full_text: After penetration tests, validate that security monitoring and controls can detect the attack techniques used. Update detection rules as needed.

controls.title: Perform Periodic Internal Penetration Tests
controls.description: Perform periodic internal penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually.
controls.full_text: Conduct internal penetration tests at least annually simulating insider threats and lateral movement. Test network segmentation effectiveness.
